certificate.moduleName: SUSE Linux Enterprise Libgcrypt Cryptographic Module
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When operated in approved mode. When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11 of the Security Policy
certificate.description: SUSE Linux Enterprise Libgcrypt Cryptographic Module is a general purpose cryptographic library based on the code from GnuPG.
testedConfigurations: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 on PowerVM (VIOS 3.1.4.00) running on IBM Power E1080 (9080-HEX) with Power10 processor with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 on PowerVM (VIOS 3.1.4.00) running on IBM Power E1080 (9080-HEX) with Power10 processor without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE G242-P32-QZ with ARM Ampere(R) Altra(R) Q80-30 processor with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE G242-P32-QZ with ARM Ampere(R) Altra(R) Q80-30 processor without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE R181-Z90-00 with AMD EPYC(TM) 7371 processor with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on GIGABYTE R181-Z90-00 with AMD EPYC(TM) 7371 processor without PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on IBM z/15 with z15 processor with PAI SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on IBM z/15 with z15 processor without PAI SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on Supermicro Super Server SYS-6019P-WTR with Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4215R processor with PAA SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP4 running on Supermicro Super Server SYS-6019P-WTR with Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4215R processor without PAA

SP 800-90B compliant Non-Physical Entropy Source (ESV cert. E31)
The Libgcrypt CPU Time Jitter RNG version 3.4.0 entropy source (with SHA-3 as the vetted conditioning component) is located within the module’s cryptographic boundary.
KAT AES ECB mode with 128, 192 and 256-bits keys, encryption, and decryption (separately tested).
KAT AES CMAC with 128-bit key, MAC generation.

KAT CTR_DRBG with AES with 128-bit key with DF, with and without PR. KAT Hash_DRBG with SHA-256 with and without PR. KAT Hash_DRBG with SHA-1 without PR. KAT HMAC_DRBG with HMAC-SHA-256 with and without PR. Health tests according to section 11.3 of [SP800-90Arev1]
NIST SP800-90B Entropy source start-up test RCT and APT with 1024 samples
NIST SP800-90B Entropy source continuous test: RCT with Cutoff C = 31; APT with Cutoff C = 325; W = 512
KAT ECDSA signature generation and verification with P-256 and SHA-256 (separately tested).
KAT HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC-SHA-224, HMAC-SHA-256, HMAC-SHA- 384, HMAC-SHA-512. KAT HMAC-SHA3-224, HMAC-SHA3-256, HMAC-SHA3-384, HMAC-SHA3-512.
KAT RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 signature generation and verification with 2048-bit key and SHA-256 (separately tested).
KAT SHA-1, SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384 and SHA-512.
KAT PBKDF2 with SHA-1 and SHA-256.
PCT using signature generation and verification with SHA-256.
PCT using signature generation and verification with SHA-256.
Failure of pre-operational tests or conditional tests.
An error message related to the cause of the failure.
Random numbers are requested in the error state or cipher operations are requested on a deallocated handle.
